topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The delivery arrived just as I was finishing up my evening routine. A plain brown box sat on my doorstep, addressed clearly to the unit above mine, Apartment 3B. My brow furrowed as I picked it up. The handwriting on the label was neat, almost too perfect. I glanced around the hallway, the overhead light casting long, stark shadows that seemed to deepen the quiet of the building. I tapped the cardboard lightly with my fingernail, a small, dry sound in the stillness. Why would someone send a package meant for another resident? A flicker of unease tightened my chest. I carried it inside, placing it on the kitchen counter, and spent the next hour pacing, my gaze repeatedly drifting toward the door of 3B, waiting for anything to happen.

[story 2]

When the courier handed me the parcel, the exchange was brief and impersonal. I took the box, my fingers brushing against the slightly rough texture of the packing tape. The address on the exterior clearly indicated it was for the former occupant. A cold knot formed in my stomach. I examined the packaging closely, noticing a specific type of tape that felt unusually industrial. I retreated to my apartment, locking the door behind me with unnecessary force. I circled the box several times, my breath catching in my throat with each rotation. I couldn't shake the feeling that this delivery was not just an oversight; it felt deliberate, a misplaced piece of someone else's life thrust into mine.

[story 3]

I found the package tucked beside the recycling bin. It was addressed to the apartment above, a clear indication that it wasn't meant for me. As I held it, a distinct tension coiled in my shoulders. I didn't immediately open it. Instead, I walked to the window, peering down at the street below, my posture rigid. Every passing car seemed to feel like an intrusion. My mind raced, cataloging possibilities—a prank, a mistake, something more intricate. I turned back to the box, my movements slow and deliberate, as if handling it required extreme caution. The silence in my small living room amplified the sound of my own quickened pulse.
